codeReview.summary: The rerun fix resolves the two prior oversized-test blockers: commit `b6b1367` removes the three over-limit suites, registers ten focused replacements in `tests/unit/run-unit-tests.ts:17-20,31-36,77-93`, and every replacement file in the commit is within the 200-line cap (29-138 lines). The feature still fails review overall because the same commit bundles unrelated version bumps in `package.json` and both extension manifests, which mission AGENTS forbids for non-release refactor work.

issues.description: Although the line-limit fix itself is complete, commit `b6b1367` also bumps the package version from `0.4.61` to `0.4.62`. Mission AGENTS marks package/manifest version bumps as out of scope for non-release refactor features, so this introduces a new blocking scope violation unrelated to splitting test files.

The same test-only fix commit also bumps the Chrome manifest version from `0.4.61` to `0.4.62`. That metadata change is unrelated to resolving the oversized profile/JSON-RPC test files and is explicitly prohibited by mission guidance for refactor features.

The Firefox manifest version is bumped in the fix commit as well, repeating the same forbidden release-metadata side effect. Because the feature scope was limited to splitting oversized test files, this remains a blocking scrutiny issue even though the file-size defects are fixed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `refactor-worker` skill's completion contract is still not being enforced consistently. This handoff records `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true` even though its own verification block shows `npm run lint` exited 1 and does not record a passing `check:repo-standards`, while the skill says to mark `followedProcedure` true only after all validation steps passed and were recorded.

Shared command state still omits `check:repo-standards` even though both repo AGENTS and the `refactor-worker` skill require it as a final validation step. That omission may be contributing to workers under-reporting or skipping this guardrail in handoffs.

summary: The fix does what it was meant to do for the original blockers: the oversized profile and JSON-RPC test suites are split into ten focused modules and all committed replacements are under 200 lines. Review still fails because commit `b6b1367` introduces new blocking scope violations by bumping `package.json` plus both Chrome/Firefox manifest versions in a test-only refactor feature.
